concerns.text: The regression step (step 6) fits a joint linear/polynomial/logarithmic model with crossing number and braid index as separate predictors, but the plan does not address the deterministic mathematical constraint braid_index ≤ crossing_number. This multicollinearity threatens coefficient interpretability; the plan should either (a) model braid index as a function of crossing number first, (b) use dimensionality‑reduction (e.g., principal components), or (c) adopt regularisation (ridge) and clearly state that coefficients are not causal effects.

The exploratory analysis (step 5) stratifies plots by alternating/non‑alternating status but the subsequent regression models (step 6) do not include alternating classification as a covariate or interaction term, leaving a potential confound that could bias the relationship between the topological invariants and hyperbolic volume.

Filtering to hyperbolic knots (FR‑012) changes the scope from “all prime knots ≤ 13 crossings” to “hyperbolic prime knots only”. While the plan acknowledges a selection‑bias limitation, it does not propose any sensitivity analysis or explicit statement of how this restriction affects the generalisability of the findings to the full prime‑knot population.
